steps.title: Read the underlined sentence on its own
steps.content: It says the team used data from 1994 to 2010 for a set of US companies and compared over 29,000 annual earnings forecasts to the actual earnings reported. That is a description of what data was gathered and what was done with it — the procedure.

steps.title: Check where it sits in the paragraph
steps.content: The first sentence states the study's claim; the underlined sentence explains how the study was carried out; the final sentence reports what the team found. So the underlined sentence's job is to lay out part of the method.

steps.title: Eliminate by rule
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To summarize the results': the results appear in the next sentence (greater sunshine, more inflated forecasts), not in the underlined one, which only describes data and comparison. Misread. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To present a specific example that illustrates the study's findings': no single company or forecast is given as an instance — the sentence reports the dataset, not an example. Overreach. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To explain part of the methodology used in the team's study': matches exactly — what data, what time span, what was compared. ✓
  • Choice D — 'To call out a challenge the team faced': nothing in the sentence flags a difficulty or limitation; it is a neutral description of the procedure. Misread. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
